title: Lean4 proof of max_unreduced_additions overflow safety
description:
Prove in Lean4 that `max_unreduced_additions` (gfp/mod.rs:402) correctly computes the maximum k such that k * (P-1)² ≤ u128::MAX.

The function is already extracted to `proofs/Gf2Core/Funs.lean` via Charon/Aeneas. Needs a theorem in the existing proof infrastructure.

Properties to prove for all ValidPrime P:
1. Overflow bound: `k * (P-1)² ≤ u128::MAX`
2. Value equality: `k = min(u128::MAX / (P-1)², usize::MAX)` — the function returns a `usize`, so the result is clamped to `usize::MAX` when the u128 division exceeds the usize range.
3. The unclamped division satisfies `(u128::MAX / (P-1)²) * (P-1)² ≤ u128::MAX`

This was originally scoped under the Kani issue (f9819a76) but is a natural Lean4 proof target since it's pure const-generic arithmetic with no heap allocation.
